plan_metadata.name: Teams Agent With Data Custom API Azure OpenAI ts Remote Debug

steps.description: Click the "Teams Agents and Apps" option within the dropdown menu under the "New Project" dialog in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the "General Teams Agent" option in the dropdown list within the "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" selection menu.
Click the "Custom API Preview" option in the dropdown menu under "Select an option to load your data" within the Teams Agent with Data interface.
Click the text input field labeled "Enter OpenAPI Description or Document Location" in the top bar of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click inside the input field labeled "Enter OpenAPI Description Document URL" in the VS Code extension interface header to initiate document entry.

Type the URL 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SLdragon/example-openapi-spec/main/real-no-auth.yaml' into the 'Enter OpenAPI Description Document URL' input field within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Press the 'Enter' key to confirm the input URL displayed in the OpenAPI Description Document field within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Toggle all checkboxes" option in the dropdown menu under the search field labeled "Select Operation(s) Teams Can Interact with" in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the dropdown arrow in the "Select Operation(s) Teams Can Interact with" menu to expand selection options.
Click the "TypeScript" option from the programming language dropdown in the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click on the "Default folder" dropdown in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select the folder location for your project.
